query: What is the most recent World Bank / Argus Media monthly price of liquid sulphur Tampa in USD per metric ton for 2026? What were the values month by month January through June 2026?
answer:
## Research Complete: Liquid Sulphur (Tampa) Monthly Price, 2026

### Key findings

The **World Bank Pink Sheet "Sulphur (Tampa)"** series is reported in **USD/metric ton** and is based on the **quarterly Argus/Green Markets Tampa molten sulphur contract**. Because the underlying benchmark settles quarterly, the Pink Sheet repeats the same value across all three months of a quarter.

**Month-by-month, January–June 2026 (implied Pink Sheet $/mt):**

| Month | Underlying Tampa contract | Pink Sheet value (~$/mt) |
|---|---|---|
| Jan 2026 | Q1: $495.69/lt CFR | **~$488** |
| Feb 2026 | Q1: $495.69/lt | **~$488** |
| Mar 2026 | Q1: $495.69/lt | **~$488** |
| Apr 2026 | Q2: $655/lt (record) | **~$645** |
| May 2026 | Q2: $655/lt | **~$645** |
| Jun 2026 | Q2: $655/lt | **~$645** |

**Most recent value (June 2026):** ~$645–655/mt, reflecting the record Q2 Tampa settlement.

### Confirmed anchor figures
- **Q1 2026 Tampa contract: $495.69/long tonne CFR** (Green Markets)
- **Q2 2026 Tampa contract: $655/long tonne delivered — a new record** (Argus)
- Context: Q4 2025 was $310/lt; Q3 2025 ~$252/lt

### Important caveat
I could **not** directly text-extract the exact published World Bank Pink Sheet $/mt figures (the PDF/Excel files aren't machine-readable through my tools). The values above are derived from the confirmed underlying Argus/Green Markets Tampa contract settlements, converted long-tonne → metric ton (÷1.01605). The World Bank's published figures may differ by a few dollars depending on its exact conversion/rounding, so treat Q1 as ~$488–496/mt and Q2 as ~$645–655/mt.

### Market driver
The Q2 2026 record was driven by the **Iran war / Strait of Hormuz disruption** that halted Middle East supply — part of a broad 2026 rally (FOB Middle East spot rose from ~$525/t in Feb to ~$900/t in June).

Full sourcing and structured data points have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

key_facts: World Bank Pink Sheet 'Sulphur (Tampa)' is a monthly series in USD/metric ton, derived from the quarterly Argus/Green Markets Tampa molten (liquid) sulphur contract; the same value is repeated across all three months of each quarter.
Q1 2026 (Jan-Mar) Tampa molten sulphur contract settled at $495.69/long tonne CFR (Green Markets), implying a Pink Sheet value of roughly $488/mt for January, February, and March 2026.
Q2 2026 (Apr-Jun) Tampa sulfur contract settled at a RECORD $655/long tonne delivered (Argus), implying a Pink Sheet value of roughly $645/mt for April, May, and June 2026.
The Q2 2026 Tampa contract 'settled at a record in April,' driven by the Iran war / Strait of Hormuz disruption halting Middle East sulphur supply.
Prior quarterly Tampa settlements: Q4 2025 = $310/lt delivered; Q3 2025 ≈ $252/lt.
US Gulf sulphur averaged ~$540/MT in Q1 2026 per ChemAnalyst, up ~26% quarter-over-quarter.
Global spot sulphur surged in 2026: FOB Middle East from ~$525/t (Feb) to ~$900/t (June); CFR China from ~$546/t (Feb) to ~$1,075/t (June).
The most recent 2026 monthly value (June, published in the July 2026 Pink Sheet) reflects the Q2 record settlement of ~$645-655/mt.

Exact World Bank published $/mt figures could not be text-extracted from the PDF/Excel Pink Sheet files; values shown are derived from the underlying Argus contract settlements and lt-to-mt conversion (1 lt = 1.01605 mt).
